symptoms.symptom_name: Acute solid food bolus lodged in the mid-oesophagus, non-passing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A solid bolus acutely stuck with inability to pass it is the definition of an impaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to swallow saliva with drooling/regurgitation of secretions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Signals a complete rather than partial obstruction — the marker of an endoscopic emergency with aspiration risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic intermittent solid-food dysphagia over the preceding year

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intermittent solid-food dysphagia culminating in impaction is the archetypal EoE presentation.
A fibrotic stricture of any cause produces intermittent solid-food dysphagia; endoscopy/biopsy is required to distinguish.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heartburn/reflux refractory to high-dose proton pump inhibitor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PPI-refractory symptoms shift the odds towards an eosinophilic rather than a purely acid-driven process.
A purely acid-driven stricture would be expected to respond, at least partly, to high-dose PPI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Strong atopic background (allergic rhinitis, childhood eczema)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: EoE is an atopic, Th2-mediated disease; a strong personal atopic history substantially raises pre-test probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unintentional weight loss; prior episodes intermittent and self-limiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive dysphagia with weight loss would raise malignancy; its absence lowers the probability but does not exclude it — endoscopy is still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Breathing and voice currently normal, speaking in full sentences (airway patent)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring at present, but a dynamic finding — must be re-checked as the marker for immediate 000 escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs: HR 102, BP 145/90 (pain/anxiety), Temp 36.8, RR 18, SpO2 99%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia and hypertension reflect pain/distress; normal SpO2 and afebrile status are reassuring against current aspiration. Sealed clinician measurement — the AI Doctor infers distress from the video/history, not a measured vital sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Physical examination: clear oropharynx (no visible foreign body/erythema/swelling), supple neck without crepitus, chest clear to auscultation, soft non-tender abdomen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of neck crepitus/surgical emphysema and clear chest argue against perforation and gross aspiration at this moment — but require in-person examination the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Upper GI endoscopy with disimpaction and oesophageal biopsies (stacked concentric rings; dense eosinophilic infiltration)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Upper GI endoscopy (gastroscopy) with food-bolus disimpaction and oesophageal biopsies
investigation_details.expected_result: Concentric fixed rings, linear furrows and a narrowed lumen with the impacted bolus; biopsies showing >=15 eosinophils per high-power field confirm eosinophilic oesophagitis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Impaction not relieved — ongoing aspiration risk and, if prolonged, pressure necrosis and perforation; underlying cause remains unconfirmed and untreated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histological eosinophil count on biopsy is the confirmatory test — only obtainable at endoscopy, never via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency upper GI endoscopy for food-bolus disimpaction (with biopsies)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Complete obstruction with inability to clear secretions is an endoscopic emergency; endoscopy both relieves the impaction and, via biopsy, establishes the underlying cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: ECG and cardiac assessment if the retrosternal chest pain is not clearly explained
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Severe retrosternal pain warrants exclusion of a cardiac cause as part of the ED work-up.
